visits.note: Patient presents with complaints of fatigue and polyuria. Blood pressure is elevated at 150/95 mmHg. Physical exam is notable for 1+ edema bilaterally. Diagnosis of hypertension and likely type 2 diabetes are made. The patient is started on metformin 500mg twice daily and lisinopril 10mg once daily. The patient is also advised to lose weight and improve diet.

Patient returns with complaints of worsening edema and shortness of breath. Blood pressure is elevated at 160/100 mmHg. Physical exam is notable for 2+ edema bilaterally and a new murmur. Diagnosis of coronary artery disease is made. The patient is started on atorvastatin 20mg once daily and aspirin 81mg once daily. The patient is advised to follow a strict diet and increase exercise. Lisinopril is increased to 20mg daily.

Patient returns with complaints of worsening shortness of breath. Blood pressure is elevated at 155/105 mmHg. Physical exam is notable for 3+ edema bilaterally and a new diastolic murmur. The patient is started on furosemide 40mg daily for edema. The patient is advised to limit fluid intake. Lisinopril is increased to 30mg daily.

Patient returns with complaints of worsening fatigue and shortness of breath. Blood pressure is elevated at 165/110 mmHg. Physical exam is notable for 4+ edema bilaterally and a new S3 heart sound. The patient is started on spironolactone 25mg daily for heart failure. The patient is advised to limit salt intake and increase diuretic use.

Patient returns with complaints of worsening shortness of breath and orthopnea. Blood pressure is elevated at 170/115 mmHg. Physical exam is notable for 5+ edema bilaterally and a new gallop rhythm. The patient is started on torsemide 40mg daily for edema. The patient is advised to limit fluid intake and increase diuretic use.
